description_long: This is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rounded, rectangular wooden body and a slightly domed top. The front face features a cream-colored speaker grille above a control panel with two large circular dials on either side, flanking a horizontal frequency indicator with labeled bands. The wood exterior shows signs of age and wear, with a rich brown finish and some faded or scuffed areas. Both sides of the radio have vertical slatted vents, likely for additional speaker output or ventilation. A braided power cord with a two-prong plug extends from the back, which also has a small perforated panel for heat dissipation. The overall design is characteristic of mid-20th-century radios, blending functionality with a classic, decorative appearance.
description: A vintage wooden tabletop radio receiver with a domed top, cream speaker grille, dual dials, and a braided power cord. The exterior is brown with visible wear and side vents.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cream speaker grille, two large dials, horizontal frequency indicator, and decorative trim.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the curved wooden body, vertical slatted vent, and the braided power cord extending from the back.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain back panel with a small perforated section for ventilation and the attached power cord with plug.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with another vertical slatted vent and a clear view of the cord and plug.
